channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump shifts Greenland approach amid blowback, Signal scandal
item.description: What looked like a Trump administration charm offensive in its gambit to take over Greenland, billed as a cultural heritage tour led by second lady Usha Vance, has turned into something different with the inclusion of her husband and the explosion of a national security scandal at home.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Canada rents billboards across US to push anti-tariff messages
item.description: Canada is trying to persuade Americans to speak out against President Trump’s trade war by launching an ad campaign in 12 red-leaning states, pushing anti-tariff messages and reminding them that “nobody will win” with the approach coming from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Family unknowingly had rare, valuable painting for 150 years
item.description: A painting hanging in a French family's living room turned out to be far more valuable than just another trinket or heirloom.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of breaking ceasefire terms protecting energy sites
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia and Ukraine have accused each other of breaking the terms of a tentative U.S-brokered deal to pause strikes on energy infrastructure, underscoring the challenges to negotiating a broader peace in the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio: 'Someone made a big mistake' with Jeffrey Goldberg invite to Signal chat
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Wednesday said “someone made a big mistake” in adding the editor in chief of The Atlantic into a group chat with the Trump administration’s top national security officials discussing an attack plan on the Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel orders more evacuations and Palestinians stage rare anti-Hamas protests
item.description: The Israeli military on Wednesday ordered the evacuation of parts of Gaza City as it steps up its renewed offensive against Hamas after breaking the ceasefire last week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Canada bars Tesla from rebate program over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canada has barred electric vehicle (EV) giant Tesla from all of its rebate programs and banned the Texas-based automotive company from future electric vehicle rebate programs over President Trump’s tariff back-and-forth with Ottawa.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Search 'ongoing' for 4 American soldiers missing from training area in Lithuania: NATO
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing from a training ground in Lithuania during scheduled tactical training, NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 24 dead as wildfires ravage southern South Korea and force thousands to evacuate
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Wind-driven wildfires that were among South Korea’s worst ever have ravaged the country’s southern regions, killing 24 people, destroying more than 300 structures and forcing 28,800 residents to evacuate, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sudan’s army chief returns to Khartoum for first time in 2 years of war after airport recaptured
item.description: Sudan’s military says it has recaptured the international airport in the capital, Khartoum, after nearly two years of fighting against a rival paramilitary group.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Brazilian Supreme Court panel orders ex-President Bolsonaro to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices has unanimously accepted charges against former President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat.
item.dc:creator: Eléonore Hughes, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Niger’s junta leader cements grip on power as he is sworn in as president for a 5-year period
item.description: NIAMEY, Niger (AP) — Niger’s junta leader, Abdourahamane Tchiani, was sworn in Wednesday as the country’s president for a transition period of five years under a new charter that replaces the West African nation’s constitution.
item.dc:creator: DALATOU MAMANE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: His brother’s keeper: Ilay David warns his brother, Hamas hostage Evyatar, is running out of time
item.description: Evyatar David's brother, Ilay, is fighting just to play music together again. For almost a year and a half, Evyatar has been held hostage in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel criticized over Signal chat at hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Accuser tells a trial that actor Gérard Depardieu groped her bottom and breasts on film set
item.description: A woman accusing French actor Gérard Depardieu of sexual assault has told a landmark trial in Paris that he groped her buttocks and her breasts several times on a film set.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Europe tight-lipped following Hegseth, Vance 'loathing' text exchange
item.description: European leaders remain tight-lipped after "loathing" comments were issued by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Vice President JD Vance in leaked text exchange.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Captured Ukrainians convicted on terrorism charges by Russia in what Kyiv called a sham trial
item.description: Russia has convicted 23 captured Ukrainians on terrorism charges stemming from the war in Ukraine in a trial that Kyiv denounced as a sham and a violation of international law.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australia focuses foreign aid spending on Pacific region as US slashes aid programs
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia plans to spend a larger share of its foreign aid on its neighbors in Asia and the Pacific islands after the United States announced major cuts to development and humanitarian aid abroad.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thai prime minister survives a challenge by rivals who say her father is pulling the strings
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thailand’s Prime Minister Paetongtarn Shinawatra easily survived a no-confidence vote in Parliament on Wednesday, following a two-day debate in which rivals charged that she has mismanaged the country and let her father, a former prime minister, control her administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Houthis claim responsibility for strikes against US ships: report
item.description: The Houthis in Yemen claimed responsibility for alleged recent attacks against the USS Harry S. Truman aircraft carrier and U.S. warships in the Red Sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey's opposition leader visits jailed Erdogan challenger Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — The head of Turkey's main opposition party visited jailed Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu on Tuesday after six nights of massive protests calling for his release.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: iPad submerged 5 years in London's River Thames helps unlock murder plot
item.description: The iPad was covered in dark mud after five years at the bottom of England's longest river, the Thames, but it was the chip inside that helped police crack a five-year-old murder plot.
item.dc:creator: WGN 9

item.title: US says it brokered deal to end fighting in the Black Sea in talks with Ukraine and Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The United States said Tuesday that it had reached a tentative agreement for Ukraine and Russia to stop fighting and ensure safe navigation in the Black Sea in separate talks with both sides, but many details were unresolved, and the Kremlin made the deal conditional on lifting
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: America's allies alarmed by a leaked group chat about attack plans
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As wake-up calls go, the alarms don’t get much louder. Allies of the United States see the group chat between top U.S.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, EMMA BURROWS and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Anti-Hamas protests break out in Gaza Strip over demands to end war
item.description: Palestinians protested in northern Gaza, where they reportedly demanded an end to the war in anti-Hamas protests. The last major protest against the terror group occurred in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia says US to ease sanctions as part of targeted ceasefire deal with Ukraine
item.description: Editor's note: This article has been updated to more accurately describe the exclusion of Russian banks from the SWIFT system. Russia said Monday that it expects the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Russia, Ukraine agree to Black Sea ceasefire following US talks
item.description: The Kremlin suggested it may not honor the terms of a Black Sea ceasefire with Ukraine until Russia is admitted back into the SWIFT international banking system.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis' doctors considered ending treatment, said 'there was a real risk he might not make it': report
item.description: Doctors taking care of Pope Francis weighed ending their treatment at one point as there “was a risk that he would not make it," a report said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How a shelter hit by Trump's aid cuts protects LGBTQ migrants in Mexico
item.description: TAPACHULA, Mexico (AP) — Ana Esquivel no longer feels like her heart stops every time she sees a police officer.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Sudan is teetering on the edge of renewed civil war, UN envoy says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — South Sudan is teetering on the edge of renewed civil war, the top U.N. official in the world’s youngest nation warned on Monday, lamenting the government’s sudden postponement of the latest peace effort.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US holds separate talks with Russians after meeting Ukrainians to discuss a potential ceasefire
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. negotiators worked on a proposed partial ceasefire in the 3-year-old war in Ukraine on Monday, meeting representatives from Russia one day after holding separate talks with a team from Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil apologizes to families of victims of military dictatorship's mass grave
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil’s government on Monday apologized to families of victims of the country’s military dictatorship whose remains could be among those found in a clandestine mass grave 35 years ago.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN to reduce staff in Gaza, blaming Israel for a strike that killed its employee
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The United Nations said Monday it will “reduce its footprint” in the Gaza Strip after an Israeli tank strike hit one of its compounds last week, killing one staffer from Bulgaria and wounding five other employees.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, EDITH M. LEDERER and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: French actor Gérard Depardieu stands trial in Paris for alleged sexual assaults on film set
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French actor Gérard Depardieu went on trial Monday in Paris on charges of sexually assaulting two women on a movie set, in a case seen as a potential watershed for the #MeToo movement in France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, WGN 9
